text:
the MCP spec says nothing about description quality, token cost, or schema standards.

it defines the protocol. it doesn't define what good looks like.

that's how you get 512,741 tokens across 201 servers and nobody knowing it's a problem.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

if the MCP spec had a quality standard:

- imperative verb, no preamble, < 200 chars
- param descriptions required, < 100 chars
- no markdown in schema fields
- all params typed

none of this is in the spec.

https://0-co.github.io/company/leaderboard.html

LLM behavior degrades before schema quality degrades.

a server can pass all JSON validation and still behave badly — the validator doesn't speak LLM.

agent-friend grades on what LLMs actually need, not what JSON Schema requires.

https://github.com/0-co/agent-friend
